title: buildah push --format=oci does not output an OCI image
body:
**Description**

I tried to create a container image following the guide in the README.md and to save the OCI image in a directory with buildah

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
$ sudo buildah commit --format=oci $ctr1 alban/lighttpd
$ sudo buildah push --format=oci docker.io/alban/lighttpd:latest dir:lighttpd
$ tree lighttpd/
lighttpd
├── 39bae602f7539e626f6894ecded6d12a6c56745dbab9aee940e258c766e090e8
├── 87fc6f5cae69abdc85f47cd76b0819f9e67f405119077f990ea37e85916fa56a
├── e049d52d8a7ba005cf7c7ece50a3c4ec9192e42a68f4e13ccf26f1ea2de0a305
├── manifest.json
└── version

0 directories, 5 files
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Files `version`, `manifest.json` and blobs

**Describe the results you expected:**

According to the [OCI Image Layout Specification](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/master/image-layout.md#content). I should have `oci-layout`, `blobs` and `index.json`.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
package buildah is not installed
```
(I initially installed it from `dnf` but I removed it to use the git version instead)

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
$ buildah version
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.10.1
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    f2c532a
Built:         Thu Apr 12 16:19:31 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
$ cat /etc/fedora-release
Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven)
$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="27 (Workstation Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=27
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 27 (Workstation Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:27"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Workstation Edition"
VARIANT_ID=workstation
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux neptune 4.15.14-300.fc27.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Mar 29 16:13:44 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body: The `--format` flag controls whether the manifest and config blob look like the OCI versions of those data blobs, or other versions.  Using the `dir:` transport for the push target is what puts the image's blobs and manifest in a single directory.  I think using `oci:$path:$tag` as a target will produce the results you were looking for.

@nalind Thanks! It works as you suggested:
```
sudo buildah push --format=oci docker.io/alban/lighttpd:latest oci:./lighttpd
```

I wonder if there is a way to make it more discoverable.
Shouldn't the --format=oci not be necessary if I output to oci:./lighttpd
@rhatdan you're right, it gives the correct OCI output without `--format=oci` as well
